I'm going to tell you exactly
what it takes to start and grow

your business in under 10
minutes. You ready?

The seven step framework I'm
about to show you is so freaking

simple that anyone can replicate
it from total beginners to seven
figure business owners like me.

If you're new to my corner of
the internet, welcome. I'm Alex,

an award winning marketer,
copywriter and founder of the

Copy Posse, where I teach rad
humans how to make more money

with modern marketing. You can
subscribe to my channel below

for show, don't tell marketing
strategies every single week,

and make sure to ring that bell
to be notified when my next
video goes live.

Now it goes without saying that
building a profitable business

takes time, trial and error and
effort. There is no question

about it, but it does not have
to be complicated or

overwhelming. I am telling you
right now. And honestly, the

more complex you make it, the
less likely it's going to work.
Ask me how I know.

So many business owners tend to
over complicate this whole

making money online thing. They
think things like, oh, my

business isn't working, so I
just need to fix my funnel, or

launch more offers, or post more
on social media, or run more

ads, or hire the right people,
or raise my prices or discount

my prices, and the list goes on
and on.

And sure, all those things might
help. But the truth is, building

a profitable business is so much
simpler than that, you simply

need to get your offer in front
of the customers who want to buy
it period.

One product sold to the right
people over and over again can

make you millions, and all it
takes are the seven simple

marketing strategies that I'm
going to show you today. In this

video, I'm revealing the exact
seven step profit pipeline

framework that I followed when I
first started publishing videos

right here on YouTube on
February 13, 2019, and it's the

same one that helped me build a
seven figure brand in one year.

So let's start with step number
one problem. Most people think

that you should create a product
first and then find customers to
buy it. But that is so backward.

Successful businesses start with
the problem in mind first. Now

I'll be honest. I learned this
lesson the hard way when I

started another business, like a
decade ago, with what I thought

was the perfect product; a
program teaching people how to
stay at hotels for free.

The only problem was I didn't
really think about who the

product was for, and, more
importantly, why they would want

to buy it. And surprise,
surprise, this business really

never got off the ground,
despite the product being

really, really good. And after
it was all said and done, I had

spent 10s of 1000s of dollars in
that business.

So instead of asking, what can I
create and sell? Ask yourself,

what problem can I solve and for
whom? The more specific the

problem, the easier it is to
solve.

Moving on to step number two,
research. Okay, so you've got

the problem right, but here's
where most people mess up. They

assume they know what their
audience actually wants without
asking them.

Look, just because you
identified the problem doesn't

mean you know the desired
solution your audience is

looking for, or what else
they've tried that didn't work.

When I first started the Copy
Posse, I was determined to not

make the same mistake that I had
made in my first failed

business. So I talked to my
ideal customers non freaking

stop. I chatted with them in my
Instagram DMs. I had entire back

and forth conversations with
them right here in the YouTube
comment section.

I love talking to you guys in
the comment section, and I even

surveyed them when they joined
my email list to ask them

literally exactly what they
wanted. Then I simply created

that exact solution. And because
of that, in my first launch ever

to a pretty small list of 2300
people. I made $80,000.

Here's the thing. Your audience
will tell you exactly what they

want to buy. If you just ask
them, they'll tell you their

biggest fears, their deepest
desires, and the exact words

they use to describe their
problems. So stop second

guessing and start asking. It'll
make it so much easier.

And step number three, offer.
Okay, so here's something that

maybe is going to blow your
mind. Your offer is not your

product. Let me say that again,
your offer is not your product.

Your offer is the promise or
transformation that your product

provides. It's the bridge
between where your customers are

right now and where they want to
be, and what they would pay for

to help them get from that A to
B.

Most people think that they're
selling a course or a product or

a service, but that is wrong.
You are selling a better version

of whatever it is your customers
want. You are selling the
outcome, not the process.

When I launched the Copy Posse
Launch Pad back in 2020, I

wasn't selling just an eight
week copywriting course, I was

selling you a path to quitting
your nine to five and building a

six-figure freelance business in
under a year. So you see the

difference. One is a product,
the other is a promise of
transformation.

Your offer needs to include
these four things: the problem

your customer is facing, the
promise you are making of what

is possible for them, proof that
it works, and then a price that

makes it irresistible. Get your
offer right, and everything
becomes so much easier.

All right, moving on to step
number four funnel, and no, I'm

not talking about a complicated,
47-step funnel with endless

offers and upsells and
automations that so many

marketers will tell you is the
secret to their success.

Your funnel is simply the
journey your customer takes from

who are you, to take my money.
And it really doesn't have to be
any more complicated than that.

For my first four years in
business, I followed the

simplest funnel ever. I would
get free traffic from my YouTube

or social media like this, drive
people to a landing page to sign

up for something for free, like
a lead magnet. They would opt in

for that lead magnet, then they
would get some welcome emails,

and then they would get the
sales emails for an offer

related to whatever free thing
they just signed up for. That's
it.

This simple funnel generated
millions of dollars in my

business for years. Now I
recommend that you map out your

customer journey visually like I
do, because this will really

make sure that all the pieces
fit together in a way that makes

sense. It doesn't have to be
fancy, it just has to help your
customers connect the dots.

Step number five, influence. So
here's something that nobody

wants to talk about, but it
matters more and more in this

age of AI. You need to create
trust and influence in the minds

of your customers, and this all
comes down to building a
personal brand.

And before you come at me, no, I
am not talking about trying to

be an influencer or posting
seven days a week on social

media. We tend to get so caught
up in building our businesses

and serving our clients and
creating products and trying to

get results that we totally
forget about this one thing.

People trust people more than
they trust businesses.

Remember your potential clients
and customers want to see who

you are, not just what you do or
what you sell. And one of the

easiest and best ways to do this
is to simply show up on social
media.

Being real is what actually
influences connection, and

someone purchasing from you.
Showing up is what builds trust.

You don't need a huge audience
or a ton of followers to create
trust.

I've actually posted a 10 day
Instagram story challenge here

on YouTube before, and I even
did the challenge day by day

with my mastermind members. And
honestly, the results speak for
themselves.

My members saw an average of a
207% increase in views on their

Instagram profiles. And if you
want to try that challenge for

yourself, I will link that video
for you right after this one.

Just remember, influence isn't
about being fake or forced or

showing up as some version of
you that you think you need to
show up as.

It's not about creating content
or trying to be an expert when

you're not. I'm simply talking
about being present,
approachable and available.

When your dream customers and
clients come looking, and you
better believe they're looking.

Speaking of looking, step number
six is traffic. So let's talk

about traffic, and here is what
most people get so so wrong.

They think that more traffic
always equals more money. But

again, I learned this the hard
way. Traffic alone will not get
you anything.

I've actually had content go
viral before. I'm talking about

millions of views, and honestly,
nothing really happened, because
those were not the right people.

I'd rather have 100 highly
qualified visitors to my social

media or my website than 1000s
of random ones. Quality beats
quantity every single time.

Now there are actually three
types of traffic, organic,

referred and paid. Organic
traffic is free, but it does

take time and effort, right? You
have to, you know, consistently

and frequently show up on social
media to get more free traffic

to your offers or to your
website.

That is exactly how I built my
business. In the first year, I

paid absolutely no money for
ads. I just showed up on YouTube

every single week with a new
video, and I posted that video

every single week on social
media. That's it. It was simple.

Yes, it was more work than maybe
just buying traffic, but it was
free.

Now, referred traffic is when
someone else with an audience

promotes you in exchange for a
commission. Now, while this form

of traffic is technically free,
it does require relationship
capital.

And then lastly, if you have a
budget, there is paid traffic,

aka running ads, which is
definitely an option too. My

advice here is to start simple
and then test what works and
scale from there.

Once you know what works, then
you can put more and more money

into your ad spend and bring in
a lot more qualified leads

faster than free or referred
traffic.

Step number seven is profit. Now
this is a big one. Listen

carefully, revenue alone is
nothing but a vanity metric. I

have seen businesses doing
millions in annual revenue that

struggle with cash flow, and
I've also seen businesses that

are doing like under $500,000 a
year that are more profitable

than companies that are like
two, five or 10 times their
size.

So here are the metrics that
matter more than revenue.

Customer value, aka, how much a
customer spends with you on

average. This is your total
revenue divided by the total
number of customers you have.

Next, customer acquisition cost.
In other words, how much does it

cost to acquire a new customer,
or how much do you spend on ads
for a single new customer?

And then, of course, your profit
margin, or the difference

between your revenue and the
cost of running your business.

If you do not know these
numbers, you are not running a

business, you're running an
expensive hobby that is maybe
making money sometimes.

And trust me, this, again, I've
said this a lot, is a lesson

that I had to learn the hard
way. Just track everything,

because when you track you can
improve. You can optimize, even

a 1% improvement in your
conversion rate across your

funnel, can compound into
massive profit increases.

Because at the end of the day,
profit is what allows you to

actually show up more serve more
people, create more impact, and

build the business and life you
actually want.

If you're serious about building
a profitable business that

actually works today, but you
have no freaking idea where to

start, then I recommend you try
out my free quiz.

It'll take just a few seconds,
but I'll send you a personalized

list of resources to help you

exactly where you're at right
now.

You can scan this QR code to

link in the description box
below.

And if this video helped you
think just a little bit

differently about how to build a

thumbs up below and let me know,
and drop in the comments which

next.

I'll see you next week with a

Alex. Ciao for now.

Before you go, if you liked that

You know what's officially out
